2604.09874.title: Simulating Organized Group Behavior: New Framework, Benchmark, and Analysis
2604.09874.paper_summary: Simulating how organized groups (e.g., corporations) make decisions (e.g., responding to a competitor's move) is essential for understanding real-world dynamics and could benefit relevant applications (e.g., market prediction). In this paper, we formalize this problem as a concrete research platform for group behavior understanding, providing: (1) a task definition with benchmark and evaluation criteria, (2) a structured analytical framework with a corresponding algorithm, and (3) detailed temporal and cross-group analysis. Specifically, we propose Organized Group Behavior Simulation, a task that models organized groups as collective entities from a practical perspective: given a group facing a particular situation (e.g., AI Boom), predict the decision it would take. To support this task, we present GROVE (GRoup Organizational BehaVior Evaluation), a benchmark covering 44 entities with 8,052 real-world context-decision pairs collected from Wikipedia and TechCrunch across 9 domains, with an end-to-end evaluation protocol assessing consistency, initiative, scope, magnitude, and horizon. Beyond straightforward prompting pipelines, we propose a structured analytical framework that converts collective decision-making events into an interpretable, adaptive, and traceable behavioral model, achieving stronger performance than summarization- and retrieval-based baselines. It further introduces an adapter mechanism for time-aware evolution and group-aware transfer, and traceable evidence nodes grounding each decision rule in originating historical events. Our analysis reveals temporal behavioral drift within individual groups, which the time-aware adapter effectively captures for stronger prediction, and structured cross-group similarity that enables knowledge transfer for data-scarce organizations.
2604.09874.paper_summary_zh: 模擬有組織的團體（例如，企業）如何做出決策（例如，對競爭對手的行動作出反應）對於理解現實世界的動態至關重要，並可能對相關應用（例如，市場預測）帶來好處。本文將此問題形式化為一個具體的研究平台，以理解團體行為，提供：（1）具有基準和評估標準的任務定義，（2）具有相應算法的結構化分析框架，以及（3）詳細的時間和跨團體分析。具體而言，我們提出有組織的團體行為模擬，這是一項從實際角度建模有組織團體作為集體實體的任務：給定一個面對特定情況（例如，人工智慧熱潮）的團體，預測它將採取的決策。為了支持這項任務，我們提出了GROVE（GRoup Organizational BehaVior Evaluation），這是一個基準，涵蓋44個實體，並從Wikipedia和TechCrunch收集了8,052對現實世界的上下文-決策對，跨越9個領域，並具有端到端的評估協議，評估一致性、主動性、範疇、規模和視野。除了簡單的提示管道外，我們還提出了一個結構化的分析框架，將集體決策事件轉換為可解釋的、自適應的和可追溯的行為模型，實現比基於摘要和檢索的基準更強的性能。它進一步引入了一個適配器機制，用於時間感知的演變和團體感知的轉移，以及可追溯的證據節點，將每個決策規則與源自的歷史事件相連接。我們的分析揭示了個別團體內的時間行為漂移，時間感知的適配器有效捕捉了這一點，以實現更強的預測，並且結構化的跨團體相似性使數據稀缺的組織能夠進行知識轉移。
2604.09874.author: Xinkai Zou et.al.
2604.09874.authors: Xinkai Zou, Yiming Huang, Zhuohang Wu, Jian Sha, Nan Huang, Longfei Yun, Jingbo Shang, Letian Peng
